name: CERN ROOT
description: A C++ data analysis framework developed at CERN for processing and analysing the massive datasets from particle physics experiments. The standard tool for high-energy physics.
whyUseIt: ROOT is designed to handle petabytes of data from particle detectors. It provides histogramming, curve fitting, statistical analysis, and a custom file format optimised for physics data. If you work in particle physics, you will use ROOT.
fieldPosition: Particle physics — the standard analysis framework for CERN experiments (ATLAS, CMS, LHCb, ALICE)
physicistWorkflow: Load event data, apply selection cuts, fill histograms, fit signal and background models, assess statistical significance.
supervisorExpects: Ability to write ROOT macros or Python scripts using PyROOT/uproot to analyse experimental data.
computationalImpact: Enables the analysis of billions of collision events to extract rare signals like the Higgs boson.
demoDescription: Load ATLAS open data, reconstruct the invariant mass distribution of diphoton events, fit a signal plus background model, and extract the Higgs boson mass.

vocabulary.definition: ROOT's columnar data structure for storing event-by-event physics data

beginnerMistakes: Not applying proper event selection cuts before histogramming
